next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 18

Eight hundred meters of open ground. Vel measured the distance the way she measured everything now — in what it would cost.

Four agents between her and the threshold point. The commander at the far end, standing exactly where Maren's route journal said the crossing happened, eight years ago. Two permanent garrison operatives spread across the basin floor to cover the eastern approach — their approach, because the tunnel had deposited them exactly where they'd planned, east of the Reserve's primary sight lines, and it had cost them everything they'd budgeted for stealth. The two freshly deployed agents, brought in for Kem, were cutting a wide arc from the garrison camp to close the flank.

Vel crouched behind a shelf of wind-cut rock that offered approximately nothing. The striated layers — ochre, gray, dark red — were beautiful in the way that geology was beautiful when you weren't trying to hide behind it. Eighteen inches of eroded sandstone between her and four people who had more levels in their morning warm-up routines than she had left in total.

Kem was three meters to her left, pressed flat against the basin floor. His breathing was controlled. His eyes were moving — scanning the agent positions, tracking their approach vectors, doing the tactical assessment she'd taught him to do six weeks and two hundred and eighty-eight levels ago. He was at twelve. She was at eight. Between them, twenty levels. The commander alone had more than that in her suppression reserve.

"You heard her," Vel said. The commander's words from the parley were still in the air,...
open_hooks_to_carry: The Scour as landscape: vast erosion basin, exposed rock, hostile terrain
Red Reserve permanent garrison: a commander who has guarded the site for five years, witnessed three crossings, knows what zero is — plus two freshly deployed agents for the Kem retrieval; four total

The late complication: the commander reveals the threshold has degraded — natural erosion compounded by three years of deliberate dampening; a two-person cascade may not be enough; Vel's eight-year-old calculations are outdated
Vel at 16 and Kem at 18: the numbers against them rendered in dark comedy — 'I'm finally discreet enough for the Conservatory Rite'
The tunnel approach: Kem spends 6 levels (light, reinforcement, defensive), Vel spends 6 (Perception mapping, tunnel navigation, evasion); Vel at 8, Kem at 12
The commander as mirror-Sable: competent, spent, loyal to a flawed system, guarding something she knows is real because the alternative would collapse everything
Intercut: Sable trailing three hours behind at 236, spending on tracking, making incremental decisions toward the Scour

payoffs_to_land.description: The permanent Scour garrison is confirmed — the commander has been here for years, knows the threshold intimately, and has standing orders to prevent crossings

prev_tail_excerpt:
...nded hers back and their fingers touched on the metal and neither of them made anything of it. That was the repair: not a moment, not a gesture, not a word. Just the ordinary human fact of one person handing another person water.

The journal was in her pack. Maren's handwriting on the pages, the routes marked in a cramped careful hand, the annotations crowded into margins that weren't wide enough for what she'd needed to say. Vel had been carrying it for eight years. She would carry it for two more days. And then she would be at zero, in a place without the Ledger, and Maren would be there or she wouldn't, and either way the journal would have done its work.

The road dipped where the earth had subsided over an old mine gallery. Vel stepped around the depression without thinking. Kem stepped around it after her, matching her line, the unconscious mimicry of two people who had learned each other's gait. He didn't know he was doing it. She did. It was one of the things she'd taught him without teaching him — the body learning from proximity, absorbing another person's economy of movement the way soil absorbs rain, quietly and without permission.

She checked her Ledger. Sixteen.

The Scour was two days south. Red Reserve agents, trained and armed and guarding a threshold that had been there longer than anyone alive. Four of them against two people with thirty-four levels between them. The arithmetic was the worst she'd ever faced, and she'd faced bad arithmetic for forty years.

But she'd told Kem the truth. All of it. And he was still walking.

That would have to be enough.

setups_to_plant.description: The threshold has been deliberately weakened — a two-person cascade may not generate enough energy. This creates a structural necessity for a third contributor, making Sable's arrival essential rather than convenient.

state_deltas_required: Near-zero levels vs. dangerous terrain and trained Red Reserve garrison; the late complication that the threshold has degraded

Every remaining ability becomes life-or-death; the late complication reveals the threshold has degraded and two spenders may not be enough; the Hoarded's active suppression is confirmed; Sable's approach seeded through incremental decisions
